task:
Implement the following plan:

# Fix: Orphaned APPROVED exception requests block new submissions

## Context

When an admin deletes a `VulnerabilityException` via the Vulnerability Exceptions table (DELETE `/api/vulnerability-exceptions/{id}`), the associated `VulnerabilityExceptionRequest` remains in **APPROVED** status. When a user later tries to submit a new exception request for the same vulnerability, `checkForDuplicateActiveRequest()` finds the orphaned APPROVED request and incorrectly blocks the submission with:

> "This vulnerability already has an active exception request (Status: APPROVED, Expires: ...)"

The two tables (`vulnerability_exception` and `vulnerability_exception_request`) have no FK relationship, so deleting an exception has no cascade effect on the request.

## Fix

Modify `checkForDuplicateActiveRequest()` in `VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService.kt` (line 560-572) to **verify that an actual exception still exists** for any APPROVED request it finds. If the exception was deleted, auto-cancel the orphaned request and allow the new submission.

This approach is self-healing: it fixes the data inconsistency at the point where it matters, using existing repository methods and matching logic already established in `deleteExceptionForRequest()`.

### File: `src/backendng/src/main/kotlin/com/secman/service/VulnerabilityExceptionRequestService.kt`

**Change `checkForDuplicateActiveRequest()` (lines 560-572):**

```kotlin

    val existingRequests = requestRepository.findByVulnerabilityIdAndStatusIn(vulnerabilityId, activeStatuses)

    for (existing in existingRequests) {
        if (existing.status == ExceptionRequestStatus.APPROVED) {
